title: Ralph Phase K: tt-metal SDPA kernel alignment — speed + accuracy vs bitnet.cpp

stories.title: Server sync: push local commits + confirm p150a runs baseline
stories.finding: Server reset from bb75ee7 to d743ee6 (origin/main). Baseline bench on p150a: decode_tps 73.83 t/s, p50 11.7ms, PCC 0.9820 vs HF fp32. Within noise of MEMO's 74.28 snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: Local HEAD pushed to origin/main — DONE (origin at d743ee6 after subsequent commits at 97cb9bc)
ssh TT pulls latest — DONE (server aligned via git reset --hard origin/main)
Server-side bench_batch32 runs and reports decode_tps — DONE (73.83 t/s baseline, then 74.18 after FP32_ACC default flip)

stories.title: Phase K1: unit harness + BITNET_SDPA_KERNEL_VARIANT env flag plumbing
stories.finding: SDPA RFE vs torch fp32 = 0.0257 (bf16 floor 0.0025, net TT drift 0.023). Decision gate (<=0.05) triggered: redirect kernel arc away from SDPA.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_sdpa_kernel_alignment.py exists and fixes layer-0 (Q,K,V) from pcc_localize capture — DONE
Harness compares stock fused SDPA vs torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention and reports per-head RFE + softmax-only and matmul-only RFEs — DONE
Env flag BITNET_SDPA_KERNEL_VARIANT is plumbed through _forward_prefill_with_preallocated_cache in src/bitnet_tt/layers/attention.py — DONE (stock | pytorch_ref; aligned_* reserved)
Harness reports sub-op RFE for stock kernel — DONE, sub-op RFE 0.023 < 0.05 redirect gate
pytest tests/test_sdpa_kernel_alignment.py passes on p150a — DONE (2 passed in 4.08s)

stories.title: Redirect: attn_sub_norm per-op RFE isolation (RMSNorm as dominant amplifier)
stories.finding: FP32_ACC=1 drops L0.post_input_norm 0.009→0.003 (-68%), L0.post_attn_sub_norm 0.420→0.414 (-1.4%), L0.post_self_attn 0.240→0.235 (-2%). Conclusion: RMSNorm kernel benefits from fp32 acc at the first norm (where input is clean), but downstream the Q/K/V ternary matmul chain + SDPA dominates drift and absorbs the local win. Amplifier is ternary_matmul, not attn_sub_norm.
acceptanceCriteria: Re-run pcc_localize.py with BITNET_RMSNORM_FP32_ACC=1 on layer 0 and capture new post_attn_sub_norm RFE — DONE (0.414197)
Decision gate (>=30% drop): 1.4% drop, FALSIFIED. attn_sub_norm is NOT the amplifier.
Record post_attn_sub_norm RFE delta between FP32_ACC=0 and FP32_ACC=1 in progress.txt — DONE
Redirect recommendation: investigate ternary_matmul kernel drift (Q/K/V chain accounts for 0.232 of the 0.235 at post_self_attn).

stories.title: Redirect: RoPE + RMSNorm kernel compute-fidelity alignment
stories.finding: RMSNorm kernel amplifier hypothesis FALSIFIED by tests/test_rmsnorm_kernel_alignment.py. Feeding layer-0 post_self_attn drifted input (RFE 0.235) through TT rms_norm yields RFE 0.220 (amp 0.93x) — slightly REDUCES drift rather than amplifying. fp32_acc variant is essentially identical (0.220 vs 0.219, -0.3%). Session 8's 0.420 post_attn_sub_norm signal is a comparison-order artifact, not kernel amplification. No kernel edit needed; the real drift engine is the Q/K/V ternary matmul chain feeding SDPA.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify amplifier site — DONE (falsified: no single RMSNorm site amplifies)
Apply fp32 accumulator at amplifier site only — N/A (no amplifier to target); instead enabled default global fp32_acc via bitlinear.py change
bench_accuracy PCC improves — PARTIAL (+0.00005 vs HF, +0.002 vs bitnet.cpp)
decode_tps stays >= 70 t/s — DONE (73.65-74.2)

stories.title: Speed lever: decode-path hot-op review — trace profile + one realized kernel win
stories.finding: Non-trace profile top-5: ffn.sub_norm 153us, attn.sub_norm 147us, attn.rope 134us, ffn.slice_gate_up 131us, attn.create_qkv_heads 128us. All are already optimized per MEMO's dead-end list (sharded rms_norm, fused RoPE core-range split closed). Realized speed lever via FP32_ACC=1 default flip: +0.35 t/s on bench 1 run (within ~0.5 noise band). Net speed lever target (+1 t/s) not met but speed stays well above 70 floor.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile decode-path with profile_decode.py — DONE (top 5 ops recorded in progress.txt)
Propose+implement one kernel-config change — DONE (BITNET_RMSNORM_FP32_ACC default flipped OFF→ON in bitlinear.py)
decode_tps improves by >= +1 t/s — NOT MET (+0.35 within noise). Marginal, not definitive.
PCC stays within noise — DONE (0.982045 vs 0.982000, delta +0.00005)

stories.title: Phase K5: single-prompt bitnet.cpp parity + baseline variance
stories.finding: Single-prompt bitnet.cpp compare on new default (FP32_ACC=1): PCC 0.9699 (+0.002 over 0.9679 baseline), argmax=Paris match, top1 overlap 100%. 3x bench variance: 73.65/73.76/74.2 t/s (~0.5 t/s noise band). 64-prompt robustness out of scope for this Ralph session (requires new bitnet.cpp ref logits per prompt — separate data-collection task).
acceptanceCriteria: bench_vs_bitnetcpp.py on at least one prompt on new default — DONE (PCC 0.9699)
PCC vs bitnet.cpp improves by >= +0.001 — DONE (+0.002)
decode_tps >= 70 t/s on bench_batch32 — DONE (73.65-74.2 range, all >70)
Variance characterized — DONE (~0.5 t/s noise)

stories.title: Speed-recovery pass if Phase K4 drops below 70 t/s
stories.finding: N/A. Phase K arc redirected away from SDPA edits after K1 evidence; no K4 was executed. decode_tps stayed at 73.7-74.2 t/s throughout the session (well above 70 floor). Decision recorded in progress.txt.
acceptanceCriteria: decode_tps >= 70 t/s on current HEAD — DONE (73.65 minimum observed across 3 runs)
Decision recorded in progress.txt — DONE
